title: Macramé Christmas ornament set
subtitle: A set of three small macramé ornaments: a star, a snowflake, and a round bauble, in white or natural cotton cord
excerpt: Three small macramé ornaments worked from wire rings and knotted cotton cord, designed to hang on a tree or in a window. Each ornament uses only square knots and lark's heads and can be completed in under an hour. The set makes a simple handmade gift or tree decoration.
sourceNotes: Macramé ornament set synthesised from general macramé knotting practice. Small ring-based macramé ornaments are a standard beginner macramé project in the UK craft market.

glossaryTerms.definition: The standard macramé knot used throughout this set. Two working cords cross over two filler cords alternately, producing a flat, even unit.
The folded-loop mounting knot used to attach cords to the wire ring. A cord folded in half and larks-headed onto the ring produces two working ends.
A simple single loop knot used to finish cord ends and prevent unravelling. The overhand knot is tied at the tip of each ornament to secure the fringe or gathered ends.
A tight coil of cord wound around a bundle of cords to hold them together at a single point. Used to gather all the cord ends of the round bauble at the bottom of the ornament.

content.text: These three ornaments share the same materials and techniques: a wire ring base, cords attached with
lark's heads
, and
square knots

for the body. The difference between them is how the knotted sections are arranged: the star divides the cords into points, the snowflake spaces the knots in a radiating pattern, and the round bauble gathers all cords into a central
gathering wrap
. Use 2 mm cord for all three; thicker cord makes the rings look crowded at this small scale.

items.name: 2 mm white or natural cotton macramé cord
Ribbon or thin cord for hanging loops

content.text: Method
Step 1: Make the star ornament

Cut 10 lengths of cord at 50 cm each. Attach 2 lengths per point (5 points, 10 cords) to the ring with lark's head knots, spacing them evenly around the ring at 5 equal positions. For each point, gather the 4 cord ends (2 per lark's head) and tie 4 square knots to form a short hanging section. Bring the 4 ends together into an
overhand knot
at the tip of each point and trim to 1 cm below the knot. Tie a hanging loop of ribbon at the top of the ring.
Step 2: Make the snowflake ornament

Cut 12 lengths of cord at 40 cm each. Attach all 12 to the ring with lark's head knots, spacing them evenly to give 24 ends. Group the ends into 6 pairs of 4 cords. For each group, tie 2 square knots, then bring 2 cords from the left group and 2 from the right group together and tie 1 square knot in the space between adjacent groups. Trim the remaining ends to 5 cm and unravel to produce a fine fringe radiating from the ring. The fringe between the knot groups creates the snowflake arm effect.
Step 3: Make the round bauble ornament

Cut 8 lengths of cord at 60 cm each. Attach all 8 to the ring with lark's head knots, giving 16 ends. Group into 4 groups of 4 and tie 3 square knots per group below the ring. Re-group: take 2 ends from adjacent groups and tie 2 square knots per new group of 4, positioning them between the first groups. Continue with one more alternating round of square knots. Gather all 16 cord ends below the last knot round and apply a tight
gathering wrap
around all of them: wind a 30 cm separate cord around the bundle 8-10 times, then thread the end back through the wraps and pull firmly. Trim the gathered ends to 3 cm below the wrap.
Step 4: Finish all three ornaments

Attach a ribbon hanging loop to the top of each ring. Trim any uneven cord ends. To stiffen the ornaments slightly and help them hold their shape on the tree, spray lightly with a craft starch spray or diluted fabric stiffener, shape while damp, and allow to dry flat.

items.problem: The ornaments are floppy and do not hold their shape on the tree
items.cause: The 2 mm cord is too soft to hold a shape without stiffening
items.fix: Spray with fabric stiffener or a diluted mixture of PVA glue and water. Shape and pin on a padded board while damp, and allow to dry completely before hanging.

items.problem: The knots look uneven and bumpy rather than flat and regular
items.cause: The working cords were pulled at different tensions on each half of the square knot
items.fix: After each half of a square knot, pull both working cords firmly downward to seat the knot flat. Do this before starting the next half.

items.problem: The cord ends are fraying before the ornament is finished
items.cause: The cut cord ends are unravelling during working
items.fix: Wrap a small piece of masking tape around each cut end before beginning to knot. Remove the tape just before the final trim.
